The strain is available from Stephane Hacquard

(http://www.genome.gov/page.cfm?pageID=10506376)

bioprojects.title: Rhizoctonia solani AG-3 PT strain:RS-20 Genome sequencing and assembly

description.comment: Rhizoctonia solani is a soil-borne basidiomycete fungus with a necrotrophic lifestyle being classified into fourteen reproductively incompatible anastomosis groups (AGs). One of these is AG3 PT (a potato subgroup), a devastating pathogen and common inhabitant of the soil ecosystem having worldwide distribution and the availability of the genome sequence is certain to be an important resource in epidemiological and quarantine studies. A more detailed analysis of this genome and a comparative analysis with other Rhizactonia solani sub group genomes could expand our understanding of evolutionary relationships to obtain greater insights into their origins and pathogenicitiy.
description.title: Pathogen: environmental/food/other sample from Rhizoctonia solani AG-3 PT

assembly_info.assembly_method: SOAPdenovo v. 1.05; SSPACE v. 2.0; BGI GapCloser v. 2; HaploMerger v. 20111230; CAP3 v. 12/21/07; GATK v. 1.6.11; VCFtools v. 0.1.6

bioprojects.title: Rhizoctonia solani AG-8 WAC10335 Genome sequencing and assembly

description.title: Sample from Rhizoctonia solani AG-8 WAC10335

owner.name: Commonwealth Scientific and Industrial Research Organisation, Molecular Plant Pathology and Crop Genomics Laboratory, Division of Plant Industry

assembly_info.comments: This de novo assembly is of a highly heterozygous and multinucleate (cf. allopolyploid) fungal genome, which we have assembled into a representative haploid consensus. Additionally, almost all of the heterozygous mutations between homeologous chromosomes were found to be a fungal-specific type of randomized SNP mutation called RIP-mutation.
assembly_info.description: Rhizoctonia solani AG8, isolate WAC10335, Assembly version 1.0
assembly_info.submitter: Commonwealth Scientific and Industrial Research Organisation
